scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE CITY CIVIL COURT
AT BENGALURU

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Ram Kumar,
Aged about 45 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Jayanagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Suresh Patel,
Aged about 38 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Koramangala, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF MONEY LENT / ADVANCE PAID WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

2. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the cause of action arose within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Court at Bengaluru, and the Defendant carries on business / resides within such territorial limits.

3. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Court.

LIMITATION

4. The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 19 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of three years from the date of accrual of the cause of action. The suit is filed well within the prescribed limitation period.

FACTS OF THE CASE

5. The Plaintiff is a {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}} by profession and resides at Jayanagar, Bengaluru. The Defendant is a {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}} by profession and resides at Koramangala, Bengaluru. The parties are known to each other and share a friendly relationship.

6. On or about 15.03.2024, the Defendant approached the Plaintiff requesting financial assistance of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) for his personal requirements.

7. Acting on the request and based on the trust reposed in the Defendant, the Plaintiff advanced the said sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- to the Defendant on 15.03.2024 through NEFT bank transfer. The transaction bears UTR Number AXIB20240315123456. The proof of the said bank transfer is annexed herewith as Annexure A.

8. The Defendant acknowledged the receipt of the said loan amount and the liability to repay the same through electronic messages exchanged on WhatsApp on {{WHATSAPP_MESSAGE_DATE}}. The copies of the said digital communications are annexed herewith as Annexure B.

BREACH AND PARTICULARS

9. The loan was advanced on the understanding that the Defendant would repay the same on demand or within a reasonable time. The Defendant failed to repay the amount by the agreed date of default, i.e., 20.06.2024.

10. The Plaintiff made repeated oral demands upon the Defendant to repay the outstanding dues, but the Defendant procrastinated and failed to discharge the liability.

11. Consequently, the Plaintiff issued a Legal Notice dated 01.07.2024 through his counsel, calling upon the Defendant to repay the principal amount along with interest. The copy of the said Legal Notice and postal acknowledgment is annexed herewith as Annexure C.

12. Despite receipt of the said Legal Notice, the Defendant has failed to repay the amount or respond adequately. The status of the reply to the legal notice is {{LEGAL_NOTICE_REPLY_STATUS}}. The Defendant's conduct amounts to a clear breach of the obligation to repay the debt.

DAMAGES

13. The Defendant is liable to pay the principal sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- which remains outstanding and due to the Plaintiff.

14. The Plaintiff is entitled to claim interest at the rate of 12% per annum from the date of default, i.e., 20.06.2024, till the date of actual realization, as per the understanding between the parties and statutory entitlement.

15. The Plaintiff has incurred costs and suffered financial detriment due to the wrongful retention of the money by the Defendant, necessitating the filing of the present suit for recovery.

LEGAL BASIS

16. The Defendant borrowed / received a sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- from the Plaintiff and is liable to repay the same with interest under Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872.

17. The present suit is maintainable under Section 9 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

CAUSE OF ACTION

18. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on 20.06.2024 when the Defendant committed the breach / wrongful act giving rise to the Plaintiff's right to institute the present suit.

19. The cause of action further arose when the Defendant, despite receipt of the legal notice dated 01.07.2024, failed and refused to comply with the Plaintiff's demand.

20. The cause of action is not a continuing cause of action. The breach consisted of a single event. However, the Plaintiff's right to damages subsists.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

21. The suit is valued at Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs Only) for the purposes of juris
